prompt: Invent an original, vivid metaphor for the experience of learning a new language as an adult. Explain it in three sentences.
response: Learning a new language as an adult is like assembling a complex puzzle under a flickering light; you have all the pieces but must intuit their shapes and colors in the inconsistent glow, gradually piecing together the image as your eyes adjust and your patience is tested. Each new word or phrase is a piece you fit into the larger picture, sometimes finding the perfect match by chance, other times through deliberate, painstaking effort. Over time, the picture becomes clearer, revealing not just a beautiful mosaic of communication, but a deeper understanding of the world that illuminates your own life in unexpected ways.
